promotions.label: PA with GENEA accelerometer
promotions.reason: Directly applicable to core domain data processing: provides methods for physical activity classification using wrist accelerometer data, which is essential for computing the step_bin state variable in the 36-state factored MDP. Wrist accelerometry is the primary sensor modality for the project.

promotions.label: Wrist vs ankle accelerometer
promotions.reason: Core domain relevance: activity recognition methods using a single wrist or ankle accelerometer. Directly informs how the project processes wearable sensor data to infer physical activity states for the JITAI RL agent.

promotions.label: Hip and wrist algorithms
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: accelerometer algorithms for behavior classification at hip and wrist placements. Informs the sensor processing pipeline needed to derive the step_bin and activity state features from raw accelerometer data in the MDP.

promotions.label: UK Biobank accelerometer study
promotions.reason: Core domain reference: large-scale physical activity assessment using wrist accelerometers in UK Biobank (the primary data source for the project's UKB references). Demonstrates the feasibility and methodology of wrist-based PA measurement at population scale, directly relevant to the project's wearable data pipeline.

promotions.label: GENEA validation
promotions.reason: Implementation-relevant: validation of the GENEA accelerometer, which is the same sensor used in UK Biobank and relevant to the project's wearable data collection. Understanding sensor accuracy is critical for reliable step_bin and activity state estimation.

promotions.label: Smartwatch biometrics
promotions.reason: Core domain relevance: methods for smartphone and smartwatch-based biometrics using activities of daily living. Directly applicable to the project's wearable sensor processing pipeline for extracting features from smartwatch data streams.

promotions.label: Activity monitoring benchmark
promotions.reason: Implementation-relevant: provides a benchmarked dataset for activity monitoring, which is directly applicable for evaluating and comparing activity recognition algorithms used to process the project's wearable sensor data and derive MDP state features.

promotions.label: Human behavior recognition
promotions.reason: Directly applicable methodology: analysis of human behavior recognition based on acceleration data. This is the core sensor processing task -- converting raw accelerometer signals into behavior classifications that feed into the step_bin and burden dimensions of the MDP.

promotions.label: US accelerometer steps
promotions.reason: Core domain data: provides reference distributions of accelerometer-determined steps per day in US adults (Tudor-Locke et al.), directly useful for defining step_bin threshold boundaries in the 36-state MDP and calibrating the PA simulation model against population norms.

promotions.label: SSL step counts in UK Biobank
promotions.reason: Implementation-relevant and directly connected to next steps: self-supervised machine learning for step counts from UK Biobank accelerometer data. Self-supervised approaches for step counting directly relate to the project's planned sensor foundation model work (NormWear 768-dim embeddings) and improve step_bin state estimation.

promotions.label: NHANES step counting
promotions.reason: Core domain methodology: comparison of step counting algorithms for wrist accelerometry in NHANES. Directly applicable to selecting and validating the step counting algorithm used to compute the step_bin state variable from raw accelerometer data.

promotions.label: Sleep tracking accuracy
promotions.reason: Directly applicable to the sleep dimension of the MDP: evaluates accuracy of commercial sleep-tracking devices, which is essential for understanding measurement error in the sleep state variable. Sleep is one of the four core dimensions (step_bin, sleep, day_of_week, burden) of the 36-state factored MDP.
